libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use the normal generated test scaffold with temporary webapp directories and ephemeral localhost ports. If reusing the existing WebAppClassLoader test, update LoaderContext for Jetty 9.4.0.RC1: isSystemClass/isServerClass now take Class<?> and the Context interface also requires isServerResource(String, URL) and isSystemResource(String, URL). Avoid JSP or JMX-specific coverage unless the test intentionally adds the corresponding optional Jetty modules.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8856 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp:9.4.0.RC1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: JSP support is outside core jetty-webapp usage and may require extra Jetty JSP artifacts if tests exercise JSPs.
JMX coverage may require the optional jetty-jmx dependency, but it is not needed for descriptor processing, WebAppContext startup, static content, or WebAppClassLoader coverage.
The current javac failure is source incompatibility between tests written for Jetty 9.3 and the Jetty 9.4.0.RC1 WebAppClassLoader.Context interface.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is needed for meaningful org.eclipse.jetty:jetty-webapp coverage; the failure is due to a Jetty 9.4 WebAppClassLoader.Context API change, not missing setup.
